recalls.Description:
Leon Rading is recalling digital wall clocks identified by model number PH-05 due to safety concerns. These rectangular, black clocks come with a black nine-button remote control, and a yellow label on the back states "Contact for warranty! support@te-rich.com." The model number is also indicated on the white packaging. The product poses a significant risk, as the remote control contains a lithium coin battery that can be easily accessed by children, leading to potential ingestion hazards.

The recall is prompted by violations of federal regulations regarding consumer products with button cell batteries. Ingesting these batteries can lead to severe injuries, including internal chemical burns and even death. Consumers are urged to take immediate action to ensure safety and compliance with disposal regulations.
recalls.Title: Leon Rading Recalls Digital Wall Clocks Due to Serious Injury Risks from Ingestion Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For inquiries, please contact Leon Rading via email at recall@lianaocoltd.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Digital Wall Clock (front) with remote control
"Contact for warranty! support@te-rich.com" printed on yellow label on back of clock
Back of remote control with battery removed

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively at www.Amazon.com from November 2024 through January 2025 for approximately $23.

Importers.Name: Leon Rading Co. Ltd., of Owatonna, Minnesota

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to accessible lithium coin battery in remote control

Remedies.Name: Stop using the remote, remove the battery, and dispose of it properly. Mark the remote as 'RECALLED' and email a disposal photo to receive a refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Leon Rading recalls PH-05 digital wall clocks due to ingestion hazards from accessible batteries. Immediate action required for safety.
